title: Price gave me 3,000 points of profit but it turned RED. Here is why I have zero regrets.
author: Spirited_Syllabub488
selftext:
https://preview.redd.it/vp8eodep38qg1.png?width=875&format=png&auto=webp&s=8c028097c164cb05df793441a4e0132d9d9131ad

Hey guys,

Another loss to report and this one genuinely hurts to type out.

The long closed at a 2,900 point loss. But here is the part that really gets me. At one point during the trade price was up 3,000 points in our direction. I saw it and it was right there. Profit sitting on the screen staring at me and I was feeling to close the trade and book the profit but I waited and sticked with my system.

And unfortunately I closed it at a loss.

Not because I made a mistake. Not because I panicked. But because the system said close at the time window and that is exactly what I did.

I know how that sounds. I know some of you are probably thinking "why didn't you just take the profit and move on." Believe me I thought about it. That moment when you are sitting at 3,000 points of profit and you know the clock is still running is one of the hardest feelings in trading. You are not greedy. You are just human. But I have seen very big profits also, +12,000 points, and that's why I stick to my system hoping to hit such trades.

But here is the thing. I have backtested this model properly. I know how it behaves. I know its edge. And the backtest was done by closing at the time window every single time, not when it felt good, not when the profit looked nice, at the time. If I start cherry picking when to close early I am no longer trading the system I tested. I would be trading my emotions. And my emotions have no edge.

So I took the loss. And I am okay with it.

This is what systematic trading actually looks like I guess. It is not pretty. It is not always profitable in the short term. But if the system is sound and I have tested it properly then my only job is to execute it the same way every single time without doubt. The moment I start doubting it and overriding it I might as well throw the whole thing away.



The model has printed the next signal. Another **LONG**.

* **Pair :** BTC/USD 
* **Direction :** LONG / BUY 
* **Entry :** 21 Mar 2026 at 00:00 UTC 
* **Exit :** 24 Mar 2026 at 23:59 UTC 
* **SL/TP :** None, directional call only 



Am I nervous after a string of losses? Yes honestly. But am I doubting the model? No.   
I am okay to take another loss if that is what happens. This is a long term game and I am playing it that way.



**Not financial advice. Do your own research.**

comments.author: Patient_Kangaroo4864
comments.body: Unrealized PnL isn’t profit, it’s just exposure. If you don’t have predefined take-profit or trailing rules, you’re basically hoping instead of trading.

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body:
I have my model tested on different RRRs and in many ways.. But the optimal and the most profitable way is to trade it with no SL - TP and treat it with statistics. Because market volatility changes and using a fixed SL might cause some issues. Price may hit SL and then shoot in our direction but we already had the SL........

It's true to use SL and TP in our system but also we should rely on statistics and probabilities.
